items.title: Mutability, aliases and copying: runnable core task
items.prompt: Read an integer n. Make a nested list and a shallow outer copy, append n through the copy's first inner list, and print the shared inner value plus whether outer identities differ.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted draft item; independent human source, technical, pedagogy, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Start from the stated input/output contract, implement the smallest language-specific operation, and run the visible boundary before changing structure.

feedback.correct: The visible and hidden Python: Beginner to Intermediate runner checks match the declared contract.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic runner check differs; inspect input parsing, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Compiles or parses and passes every visible and hidden deterministic runner check for the declared contract.

privateAuthorNotes: Reference solution and both tests are AI-assisted draft material. Execute in the pinned runner and obtain independent human review before any exam use.

answer.referenceSolution:
import sys

def solve(data: str) -> str:
    n = int(data.strip())
    original = [[0], [1]]
    copied = original.copy()
    copied[0].append(n)
    return f"{original[0][-1]} {str(original is not copied).lower()} {str(original[0] is copied[0]).lower()}"

if __name__ == "__main__":
    print(solve(sys.stdin.read()), end="")
answer.explanation: The outer list is distinct after a shallow copy, but both outer containers still reference the same nested lists.
